description: run() is still the §8 skeleton (prints Op::plan); NO verb actually executes. Wire the real front-end: parse argv into verb + positionals + §9/§10 front-door flags (--parent/--gates/--needs, -p/--priority, -t/--tag, -m subject, --body, --as actor); resolve the §7 Binding + layout (operating checkout / tasks dir, bl-e755); inject the real clock — SystemTime → i64 unix seconds — as the SOLE home of now() generation, kept injected at the boundary so change.rs stays pure and unit-testable; construct the right BaseChange (Create/Occupancy/Update/Retire, bl-dfbd) and drive lifecycle::Engine (bl-4450) + the §6 plugin chain (bl-5d56) to seal. Covers create/claim/unclaim/update/close/drop. The one hardcoded guard (claim refuses an already-claimed ball) already lives in change.rs. Establishes the arg-parsing surface the read-verb ball reuses. Deps bl-4450/bl-dfbd/bl-5d56 are all delivered/closed (substrate exists). Without this, bl does nothing and the §17 finale (bl-0802) can't dogfood.
title: §9 CLI dispatch: wire run() → arg-parse + clock + mutating-verb execution
